headline: Moral Policing Operations Averaged 4,800 Raids Annually Since 2020
context: State religious enforcement data compiled by Sisters in Islam shows an average of 4,800 moral policing raids per year across Malaysia between 2020 and 2025.

cards.big: 4,800 moral policing raids per year across Malaysia.
cards.sub: Primary targets: khalwat (close proximity), indecent dress, and failure to attend Friday prayers.

cards.big: 87% of those charged in moral policing cases are women.
cards.sub: Offences like 'indecent dress' are enforced overwhelmingly against women, not men.

cards.big: Federal Territory religious enforcement budget is RM18M annually.
cards.sub: Spent on 180 enforcement officers. Equivalent funding could employ 120 counsellors.

cards.big: The Federal Constitution does not mandate moral policing operations.
cards.sub: Enforcement is authorised by state enactments. Standards and limits vary by state.

cards.big: 87% of charges against women. If moral standards apply equally, enforcement does not.

Religious enforcement that disproportionately targets one gender is not moral guidance. It is gendered surveillance. Reform should begin with equal application or honest acknowledgment of the disparity.
